This process is long and unhelpful to anyone needing help with legal issues. You think you are getting legal help but every lawyer you speak to has a different answer to each question asked. They spend more time focusing on what isn’t a reasonable or fair outcome and think they know what the judge will decide without even looking at the evidence given. My case was cut and dry with evidence showing my ex wasn’t following the parenting plan. He even states he no longer wants to see his child and has no intention of ever seeing him (our parenting plan shows we split custody 50/50). I tried to change the plan when he stopped seeing his son but the commissioner denied the change since he hadn’t gone a full year with no contact. It’s been 5 years since he has seen his child so I decided to file for a change again. It took 3 months to get an appt and it came after the administrative hearing my ex requested through DCS for child support. I could have used help with that hearing but they were booked out that far.
My appt was set for 12:30 but I waited until almost 2pm to be seen. The lawyer looked like a homeless woman from off the streets with no care in how she presented herself. She failed to make appropriate eye contact, had a scowl on her face at all times and was irritated anytime I asked a question, made a statement or even answered what she asked me. She was annoyed I didn’t know the legal jargon you would know if you went to law school. I had all the court documents completed but left sticky notes on them with questions I had. She asked me to give her the papers and she went through them without saying a word to me. She would read my notes and remove the sticky note to set aside. She made marks on the pages but didn’t tell me what she was doing. When I did ask her questions she spoke to me as if I was in the wrong for wanting a parenting plan that wasn’t violated each week by my ex. She told me the judge would probably order time for my son to spend with his dad because a child should spend time with both parents.
I told her my son had spent 4 years in counseling from anxiety and depression from his dad walking out of his life. He just recently accepted his loss and doesn’t blame himself anymore but wants nothing to do with his dad again. Seeing him would cause far more damage than good. But this lawyer wants to tell me the judge will probably order my ex to spend time with his child after all this? The lawyers statements were made to me as if I was trying to get even or punish his dad rather than change the legal paperwork to match what was being done anyway. And there are no penalties for violating the parenting plan all those years because I didn’t file a petition when they were violated. Yet when I did file a petition in the beginning, it was denied because my ex hadn’t violated the parenting plan for a whole year yet.
The lawyer kept telling me she understood my situation and it wasn’t fair but this was more than likely what the judge would decide. How could she say that without knowing the whole case? She didn’t even look at my file until I walked into her office that day. And everything I tried to explain to her, she didn’t seem to be listening to anyway. She was just trying to rush through it and avoid dealing with the whole thing.
Regardless of how bad this attorney was, I have had similar issues with other attorneys at Tacoma ProBono. They are there to help you fill out papers and thats it. If you ask questions you will get a different answer every time. They won’t go to court with you or represent you in any way. They are just there to state their opinions and act like they know what they are talking about. I haven’t left that office feeling confident about anything I learned from these so called attorneys. I won’t ever go back for legal advice from this place. Save yourself the time and represent yourself or fork out money for a real attorney that knows what they are doing.

When good people go through tough finical times and there is no other option than Bankruptcy, it’s imperative to have the right attorney represent you. Travis Gagnier and his dedicated team of experts at Best BK will guide you through the complex and often intimidating legal system to ensure you get the financial relief you need. Travis is a trusted and well-respected attorney who is lighting fast in getting results with creditors, the courts and the Trustee, which will bring you peace of mind and comfort for you and your family.

For our situation, Chapter 13 was the best course of action to take. Travis was a calming presence during our financial melt-down, which was rather emotional for my wife and I. He helped us get the protection we needed with a clear path to pay our creditors, which eliminated the financial strain on our family.

Do not be embarrassed or ashamed. You have legal rights to protect you and your family, so use them! Don’t go it alone, call Best BK for legal consultation to determine if Bankruptcy is right for you.

reviews.ownerResponse: In response to your review, it is noted that you have used our office on multiple occasions.  Arbitrations are by law different from mediations.  Our office bills hourly for such services. In arbitrations all documentation in support of the issues to be addressed are supplied by the parties themselves and are deemed to be accurate to the best of the supplying party’s knowledge. If a party argues the accuracy and/or authenticity of submitted materials and/or the arbitrator asks for additional clarification this will delay the ruling and add to cost. Any delay in receiving additional or corrected materials from either parties and/or if new material/documentation is deemed crucial to the proceeding there is an additional cost for review and consideration. Our office provides itemized billing statements/cc receipts for all services rendered. For arbitrations the award/ruling itself also contains the breakdown of fees/costs as well.  Statements are sent via e-mail and upon request, regular mail.

reviews.ownerResponse: In response to your review, we are sorry to hear that your experience with our office was negative.  Family law can be very litigious.  Our office strives to provide services that benefit the parties, not just check off completion of the dispute resolution process.  We treat all counsel and parties with respect   We do not advise parties, but instead explain the law, its application and our experience on presented issues with possible equitable remedies.  Pro Se litigants can be at a disadvantage and may interpret communication differently than what was explained.  Mediations that are not productive for either party are stopped to ensure that time and money are not wasted for the parties.  Our hope is that a “failed” mediation becomes one that will inspire further outside discussion with possible resolution.  We hope that the parties leave with knowledge about the law’s application in their specific issue(s) so everyone is then on equal terms and can make informed decisions.

reviews.ownerResponse: We are sorry that you believe your mediation experience was negative.  Family law is wrought with emotion & mediation can be difficult to navigate without an attorney’s guidance.  The job as mediator is to assist (not represent you) in making surer the issues presented are clear/concise and to provide to the parties an awareness the relevancy of the laws as they apply and current interpretation of how the issues are addressed within the court   Based upon this the mediator can offer alternatives/direction to the parties so they can productively negotiate and possibly reach an amicable agreement within the parties control.  Sometimes this knowledge is difficult to hear and it is difficult to change ones’ perception of how they think issues should be handled/decided/addressed.

reviews.ownerResponse:
We are truly sorry to hear how deeply dissatisfied you feel about your experience. Divorce and custody matters are among the most emotional and life-altering legal proceedings anyone can face, and we understand how overwhelming and painful that process can be.
Because of our ethical obligations and attorney-client confidentiality, we cannot discuss the specific details of any case in a public forum. However, we want to address a few important points.
Our firm is committed to providing thoughtful legal guidance, clear communication, and diligent advocacy. Mediation outcomes are ultimately decisions made by the parties involved. Our role is to provide advice, explain options, outline potential risks and benefits, and support our clients in making informed decisions. We cannot guarantee specific results, particularly in family law matters where outcomes depend on many factors, including judicial discretion and statutory guidelines.
We also take billing transparency seriously. Our clients receive detailed invoices reflecting time spent on case work, preparation, strategy discussions, document review, negotiations, and court appearances. We understand that legal fees can feel overwhelming during an already stressful time, and we work hard to ensure our billing practices are clear and consistent with our engagement agreements.
Regarding the firm name change mentioned, our rebranding was part of a broader organizational transition and not related to client feedback. We remain committed to serving our community with integrity and professionalism.
We would welcome the opportunity to speak with you directly to address your concerns and review your file together. Please contact our office so we can arrange a time to discuss this further.
We wish you peace and healing as you move forward.

I am a male and my experience with him was pathetic at best.  A couple dozen of unanswered emails, and unused pertinent information that would have been beneficial to my case.  Failed to send me a zoom meeting for a pre-trial.  Calls me while I am on vacation and asks if I were going to join the meeting.  I had to rush to my hotel and join the zoom meeting.  His paralegal even charged me a small amount when she needed to review my case when they decided to change paralegals on me.  The OC sent an email to Des explaining to him that I need to stay out of the home of my ex, that is her primary residence.  I explained that I had not been in her residence for two months.  They sent the letter two months later, the next thing I know we are in trial and I am ordered to pay $1500 of my exes lawyer fees because I had not stayed out of her house.  They had zero proof of me being in her house subsequent to the first or second letter sent to Des.  Des just did not look at the evidence and the lack thereof evidence to defend me. Des told me that was his fault and it was his responsibility to contact OC.  Des never contacted OC.  I wanted to have GAL or counselor to talk to my kids about the living situation, the OC opposed it and Des did not fight for the GAL or a counselor.  He gave up and let the ex win that request.  Even the Judge called out the fact that no GAL or counselor was used and he has nothing to go on from the children's perspective.  I provided Des information about how my kids felt regarding the living situation, Des did not use it.  It fell on def ears again.  I was forced to get a CMA for my home, Des never used it as an exhibit and used a made up value of my home that he submitted a value to OC and to the courts resulting in $45K more than the value of my home.  I asked for a mistrial, he never pursued it.  Had multiple reschedules and when I asked Des, he said it was the OC requesting the rescheduling.  I reached out to my ex asking why is her lawyer making all these reschedules, she informed me that it was my lawyer.  In my opinion, they were working together to gather as much money from "us" as they could.  I wish I would had fired him.  I spent around $60k on my divorce only to be told by Des, in my 25years, that was the second worse ruling I've ever had.  I had to ask for pre-trial conference with Des after our first trial date.  Des was totally unprepared for my case.  Had he used the information from the emails I sent him, things would have turned out better for me.  I feel Des has become complacent in the divorce world for men and doesn't really care.  He got his $60K (ish) from me and moved on.  Months later I did receive an email or phone call stating that the judge Leroy McCollough and or his bailiff failed to send Des the final judgement / ruling and it was way past the appeal date.  Maybe true, but I have my doubts because of past behavior.  He said he would still try and appeal it for me.  He did try, cost me another $1700 for their time.  I did back out of the appeal, I felt I would have lost even more.  He is good at knowing the law, he is good talker, I believe it may have been an hour of free phone call time during the almost 2 year divorce process.  Des is a nice guy, he just lost his passion.  The one thing I remember most about why I would never recommend him as a divorce lawyer to anyone that outweighs all the above.. My daughter wrote him a letter to be read in the courts and used as an exhibit (I never read the letter), well, he lost the letter that she wrote.

Natalya Maze represented me recently in a dispute with a general contractor who had done substandard work on my home. Most of his poor workmanship I dealt with myself, at my own expense, but when it became obvious after only one year that the standing seam metal roof was failing because it had been installed incorrectly, I had enough. At first the contractor lied to me and told me that he would "talk to the roofing company." Then after multiple emails he finally admitted that instead of subcontracting this roofing job to a professional roofing company specializing in metal roofs, he had one of his laborers install my roof. After two separate home inspections noted the failing roof and recommended further investigation, I hired an independent roofing consultant who confirmed that the roof was poorly installed and leaking. The general contractor then demanded to have his own roofing consultant examine the roof, and surprise surprise, even his own consultant confirmed that the roof had been poorly installed and would have to be replaced. The general contactor then demanded to re-do the work himself! (He couldn't even do it right the first time on brand new construction! There was no way I was going to have him do the work a second time!) I hired a professional metal roofing company to remove the poorly installed roof and replace it. During the job, they discovered multiple other issues, such as the bathroom fans being vented into sealed-up soffit space with no exterior vent. Where was the general contractor when this substandard work was being done?? To make a long story short(er), I hired Natayla Maze to represent me in my claim against this general contractor. Fortunately back in the very beginning of this project, I had hired Natalya to review the construction contracts before I signed them, and at that time she had modified the contract to state that if work had to be redone, I was free to hire any company to perform the work (instead of being required to let this general contractor re-do his own substandard work). I proceeded to have my roof completely replaced. Natalya endured months of back and forth with this contractor, and in the end she was able to come up with a resolution that required the contractor and his insurance company to repay me. To sum it up I will say that Natalya is an outstanding attorney. She is very good at construction disputes and she is also a very skilled negotiator who is able to remain calm even in the face of totally irrational and unprofessional opposition. In addition, she is very conscientious about the costs of legal representation and tries her best to keep costs down. I highly recommend her.

I worked on a case with Natalya, and I was amazed by the result she was able to achieve.  Natalya is very professional and knowledgeable. She is a skilled negotiator and goes the extra mile to get the best result for her clients. I look forward to working with her in the future.

The first meeting was a Zoom call. (Our choice & our first Zoom) so our daughter coached us in Zoom. We pushed the button, & after we said what we wanted, Chris had questions & we had answers.
About 1 hour & 10 minutes.
One week later we went to the Federal Way law office to meet with the lawyer son Logan Benson.
Right there, in one week was our Trust paperwork, our Will & other End of Life documents ready to be signed & notarized, which we did.
About 1 hour & 10 minutes.
We got an electronic file & a hard copy in a very nice binder presentation at this meeting.  Then it was my turn to get my assets (they did the house titles) renamed into the Trust.  I would use the documents in the PDF Will File to send, so the wording would be exact.  With some foreboding, I opened the PDF file.  Pretty sure there was work to be done separating the file documents to send to different entities.
But NO, everything was separate in stand alone PDF's ready to be used as needed. Which made my part much easier.
Both Christopher & Logan made the process so smooth & pleasant.  We've had questions & they've had answers.
It's been a huge weight of our shoulders.
Thank You
Steve & Linda Wilson
